output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Here's How to Get It Back Fast
output.meta_description: Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn your tenant rights, valid deductions, and how to demand your money back in 60 seconds.
content_outline: The Breaking Point: When Your Landlord Won't Return Your Deposit
Tenant Rights 101: How Long a Landlord Has to Return Your Deposit
What Can a Landlord Legally Deduct? (Normal Wear and Tear vs. Damage)
5 Immediate Steps to Take When Your Deposit Is Withheld
The 60-Second Solution: Sending a Security Deposit Demand Letter
What to Do If Your Landlord Still Refuses
key_points: Most states require landlords to return security deposits within 14 to 60 days after move-out.
Landlords cannot legally deduct for normal wear and tear, routine cleaning, or pre-existing damage.
A formal demand letter is often the fastest and most effective first step before escalating to small claims court.
Deposits-Back.com generates a professional, state-specific security deposit demand letter in 60 seconds for $19.99 with no signup or subscription.
output.cta: Stop waiting and start demanding. Create your professional security deposit demand letter in 60 seconds for just $19.99 at Deposits-Back.com—no sign-up, no subscription, no stress.
output.target_audience: Frustrated US renters whose landlords are withholding, not returning, or wrongfully deducting from their security deposit and who are actively searching for affordable, fast solutions to enforce their tenant rights.
